kings_guild.name: The King's Guild
kings_guild.description: Master artisans of the King's Guild each ply a different trade in a different quarter of the city. Work out who's who.
clues: Rowan belongs to the Joiners' Guild if and only if either Tanner goes with Matilda or Highrow goes with Godfrey (but not both).
Highrow, Millpond, and 11 coins go with Matilda, Rowan, and Cooper, in some order.
Weaver goes with exactly one of Beatrix, Rowan, or 7 coins.
If Godfrey belongs to the Gate Ward, then Rowan goes with Carpenter.
Exactly one of these is true: Carpenter goes with Southgate; Potter goes with Millpond; Weaver goes with 11 coins.
The Mason's levy is lower than both Beatrix and the artisan in Eastcheap.
8 coins, 9 coins, and 11 coins go with Highrow, Millpond, and Oldwall, in some order.
Tanner goes with at least two of Cedric, Millpond, and 9 coins.
The artisan in Millpond's levy is lower than both Godfrey and the Mason.
Exactly two members of the Joiners' Guild are in the South Side.
Either Weaver goes with Highrow or someone in the Ironmongers' Guild goes with Eastcheap.
Either Cedric belongs to the South Side or both members of the Joiners' Guild belong to the South Side (but not both).

Suppose Eastcheap went with 11 coins — following the clues from there hits a contradiction, so it doesn't.
chain: Start by assuming Eastcheap goes with 11 coins.
11 coins goes with Eastcheap, so Highrow can't go with 11 coins.
11 coins goes with Eastcheap, so Oldwall can't go with 11 coins.
…but now the clue “8 coins, 9 coins, and 11 coins go with Highrow, Millpond, and Oldwall, in some order” can no longer hold — a contradiction.
So Eastcheap does not go with 11 coins.

fishing.name: The Fishing Derby
fishing.description: Anglers at the lake-country derby each landed a different fish from a different pond on a different lure. Work out who caught what, and where.
clues: Wade belongs to the Sunfish Family if and only if either Perch goes with Mabel or Mossbank goes with Esther (but not both).
Mossbank, Pinehollow, and 11 lb go with Mabel, Wade, and Bluegill, in some order.
Trout goes with exactly one of Cody, Wade, or 3 lb.
If Esther belongs to the East Watershed, then Wade goes with Bass.
Exactly one of these is true: Bass goes with Willowmere; Crappie goes with Pinehollow; Trout goes with 11 lb.
The angler who landed the Carp's weight is lower than both Cody and the angler at Foxglen.
5 lb, 7 lb, and 11 lb go with Mossbank, Pinehollow, and Stillwater, in some order.
Perch goes with at least two of Dale, Pinehollow, and 7 lb.
The angler at Pinehollow's weight is lower than both Esther and the angler who landed the Carp.
Exactly two members of the Sunfish Family are in the Lowland Basin.
Either Trout goes with Mossbank or someone in the Catfish Family goes with Foxglen.
Either Dale belongs to the Lowland Basin or both members of the Sunfish Family belong to the Lowland Basin (but not both).
